title: Docker Architecture, OCI Boundaries, Isolation, and Environment Evidence · Guided Container Studio
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working container architecture and environment evidence map for docker architecture, oci boundaries, isolation, and environment evidence, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of container architecture and environment evidence map while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.
Distinguish a container, image, process, namespace, cgroup, virtual machine, and host kernel without overstating isolation.
Separate OCI image, runtime, and distribution contracts from Docker-specific implementation and product behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace docker cli, contexts, engine api, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api, keep Docker Client Daemon Api connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Docker Client Daemon Api condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Docker Engine 29 Architecture and 29.6.1 Release — Controls current Engine, API, containerd, runtime, security update, deprecation, installation, architecture, and compatibility evidence.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Client Daemon Api precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Client Daemon Api, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API.

options.text: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Docker Client Daemon Api.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Client Daemon Api for container architecture and environment evidence map.
State the Docker Client Daemon Api preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-version-context-baseline-okfav4". Tailor its architecture-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Version Context Baseline into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline, keep Docker Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-version-context-baseline-okfav4
  - competency: docker-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: restored checksum and application-level consistency query
    case-key: okfav4
    change: |
      operation: pull
      path: client -> engine-api -> daemon -> containerd -> registry
      kernel-boundary: shared-host-kernel
    verify: docker context inspect training && docker info --format json
    failure: Reject any conclusion based only on the client version or a claim that the container boots a guest kernel.
    repair: Capture client and server versions, active context, platform, runtime, storage driver, and security options separately.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Docker Version Context Baseline structural check is only one gate. container architecture and environment evidence map must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Version Context Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Capture client, server, API, context, platform, storage, security-option, and plugin versions before changing Docker state.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Version Context Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: A docker --version string proves the client is connected to the intended compatible daemon.
